mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and elevated recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, invalidating the recession risk channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation and declining real personal income growth erode household purchasing power, forcing consumers to cut discretionary spending and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals restoring supply chain capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 60d returns during recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in regime-filtered regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong residential investment demand and future economic activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases correlate with negative 60d returns in greater than 60% of observations, breaking the leading indicator relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 290, extreme price pressures trigger aggressive central bank tightening expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL greater than 290 yields positive 5d returns in greater than 50% of cases, indicating markets have fully priced in the inflation shock.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decrease in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals an imminent economic contraction, leading investors to price in lower earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP declines fail to predict negative 60d returns during late-cycle phases, with Rank IC falling below 0.02 in regime-specific tests.
